reasoning_model_1: The model successfully synthesizes the text 'Really? Quick, tell me the details!' with perfect content accuracy. The speech is fluent and natural (0:00-2:00s). Regarding the core paralinguistic feature 'high pitch', the model adopts an excited, eager tone which naturally elevates the pitch significantly compared to a neutral reading. The intonation on 'Really?' (0:00-0:50s) and 'Quick' (0:50-1:00s) reaches a high register, effectively complying with the prompt's instruction to read with a high pitch while maintaining naturalness.
reasoning_model_2: The model completely fails the content accuracy criterion. The prompt required the sentence 'Really? Quick, tell me the details!', but the audio only generates 'Details, details' (or a repetition of the last word). According to the detailed scoring standard, if the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script or the meaning is completely changed due to major errors/omissions, the score must be 0. Therefore, no further evaluation of pitch or fluency is relevant.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed all instructions, generating the correct text with the requested paralinguistic feature (high pitch/excited tone). Model 2 failed to generate the correct text, outputting only a fragment ('Details, details') instead of the full sentence. Consequently, Model 1 is the clear winner.
